heading: Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 92–203, § 18(a)85 Stat. 710Dec. 18, 1971
label: 270–1 to 270–3 Repealed. , ,Pub. L. 92–203, § 18(a)85 Stat. 710Dec. 18, 1971
notes: Section 270–1, acts , ; –(d), , authorized making of homestead allotments to native Indians, Aleuts, or Eskimos and provided for conveyance of allotted lands. Section was formerly classified to , Territories and Insular Possessions.May 17, 1906, ch. 2469, § 134 Stat. 197Aug. 2, 1956, ch. 891, § 1(a)70 Stat. 954section 357 of Title 48

Section 270–2, , as added , , permitted allotments of land in national forests if land was certified as chiefly valuable for agricultural or grazing uses. Section was formerly classified to .act May 17, 1906, ch. 2469, § 2Aug. 2, 1956, ch. 891, § 1(e)70 Stat. 954section 357a of Title 48

Section 270–3, , as added , , prohibited making of an allotment unless person made satisfactory proof of substantially continuous use and occupancy of land for five years. Section was formerly classified to .act May 27, 1906, ch. 2469, § 3Aug. 2, 1956, ch. 891, § 1(e)70 Stat. 954section 357b of Title 48
